title: Electric charge
short: A basic property of matter that causes electric force, measured in coulombs (C).
full:
Electric charge is the property of matter responsible for electrical interactions. It comes in two signs — positive and negative — with like charges repelling and unlike charges attracting. Charge is quantized (a multiple of the elementary charge e ≈ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ C) and conserved.

The force between two point charges follows Coulomb's law, F = k·q₁·q₂/r²; the charge stored on a capacitor is Q = C·U.

The SI unit is the coulomb (C); one coulomb is one ampere flowing for one second.
